title: Check Point Harmony SASE Admin Audit (Check Point)
spl:
index=checkpoint sourcetype="cp_log" product="SmartConsole" earliest=-30d
| where match(lower(operation),"(?i)create|modify|delete|publish|install")
| stats count earliest(_time) as first latest(_time) as last values(operation) as ops by administrator, object_name
| sort -last
description: Centralized SASE policy changes affect all connected users and sites. Audit logs enable compliance, change correlation, and accountability.
value: Centralized SASE policy changes affect all connected users and sites. Audit logs enable compliance, change correlation, and accountability.
implementation: Forward audit logs via Log Exporter or Smart-1 Cloud. Alert on after-hours changes or policy publishes without change ticket. Correlate access anomalies with recent policy changes.
visualization: Table (recent changes), Timeline (admin activity), Bar chart (changes by admin).
knownFalsePositives: Anomalies from new SaaS app rollouts, browser updates, or vendor URL category changes.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Check Point App for Splunk (Splunkbase 4293).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `sourcetype=cp_log` (audit/admin logs).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Forward audit logs via Log Exporter or Smart-1 Cloud. Alert on after-hours changes or policy publishes without change ticket. Correlate access anomalies with recent policy changes.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=checkpoint sourcetype="cp_log" product="SmartConsole" earliest=-30d
| where match(lower(operation),"(?i)create|modify|delete|publish|install")
| stats count earliest(_time) as first latest(_time) as last values(operation) as ops by administrator, object_name
| sort -last
```

Understanding this SPL

**Check Point Harmony SASE Admin Audit (Check Point)** — Centralized SASE policy changes affect all connected users and sites. Audit logs enable compliance, change correlation, and accountability.

Documented **Data sources**: `sourcetype=cp_log` (audit/admin logs). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Check Point App for Splunk (Splunkbase 4293). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: checkpoint; **sourcetype**: cp_log. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=checkpoint, sourcetype="cp_log", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Filters the current rows with `where match(lower(operation),"(?i)create|modify|delete|publish|install")` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by administrator, object_name** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the vendor admin console that produced the feed (see Data sources and App) for the same entities and time window.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Step 4 — Operationalize
Step 4 — Operationalize
Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (recent changes), Timeline (admin activity), Bar chart (changes by admin).
grandmaExplanation: We use network security tools to watch who accesses what, help prevent data leaks, enforce network isolation, and secure cloud and remote access.
hardware: Check Point Harmony SASE management portal, Smart-1 Cloud
cimSpl:
| tstats `summariesonly` count
  from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication
  by Authentication.user, Authentication.action, Authentication.src span=1h
| sort - count
